title: Is SoundHound Stock a Buy Based on Its $140 Billion Total Addressable Market?
text: SoundHound AI reported Q1 2026 earnings on May 7, featuring revenue that met expectations, though shares dropped sharply afterward. The company generated $44.2 million in quarterly revenue, representing a 52% year-over-year increase. The market's concern centered on expanding losses and the lack of guidance elevation. However, the company maintained its 2026 revenue projection of $225 million to $260 million and forecasted 2027 minimum revenues between $350 million and $400 million. Regarding the addressable market opportunity, SoundHound projects a $140 billion total addressable market spanning smart devices, customer service, and retail sectors. The company has secured notable partnerships, including a new Walmart agreement to integrate voice AI across the retailer's television brand. The article notes practical applications across industries: automotive drivers can order food hands-free, restaurants report an 11% increase in revenue compared to non-AI locations when implementing the technology, and hospitality providers can process bookings and payments through voice interactions. The author characterizes the recent pullback as potentially a buying opportunity for aggressive investors, though acknowledges the stock's high volatility with a beta of 2.7.
